facts.statement: Compression garments significantly reduce muscle oscillation (vibration) during impact, which decreases muscle fatigue and micro-trauma.
facts.citation: Impact of Lower Limb Compression Garments on Muscle Oscillation. Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center.

facts.statement: Compression aids in the flushing of lactate and metabolic waste during active recovery.
facts.citation: Evaluation of Compression Garments for Recovery. Journal of Applied Physiology.

facts.statement: Compression provides 'Second Skin' tactile feedback, enhancing proprioception (body awareness) and joint alignment during complex movements.
facts.citation: Influence of Compression Garments on Joint Reposition Sense. ResearchGate / IonicFire Review.

facts.statement: Reduced tissue vibration generally supports tendon and ligament stability during deep stretching.
Compression supports lymphatic return in the pelvis and legs, countering the stagnation from prolonged seated posture in the saddle.
Supports lower back and core stability for rider posture.
Graduated compression offers a 90% risk reduction in symptomless DVT for high-risk passengers on flights >4 hours.
facts.citation: Cochrane Database of Systematic Reviews. Clarke MJ et al.

facts.statement: Significant reduction in lower cell ankle edema compared to non-compression control groups.
Textured 'Micro-Massage' fabrics (like MicroPerle) stimulate superficial initial lymphatics, mimicking Manual Lymph Drainage (MLD).
Alternating compressive and decompressive forces improve skin elasticity and drainage in lipedema patients.
facts.citation: MDPI: Pilot Study on Micro-Massage Compression in Lipedema.
